summary: This lesson teaches how go verbs behave irregularly in the future (will) and conditional (would) tenses, typically contracting with a -d- (tener → tendré, venir → vendré, poner → pondré, salir → saldré). Hacer and decir are even more irregular (haré, diré). Poder also contracts but is not a go verb (podría).

teachings.conceptName: Go verbs: irregular future and conditional
teachings.summary: Go verbs contract in the future and conditional tenses. Most replace the final vowel of the infinitive with -d-: tener → tendr-, venir → vendr-, poner → pondr-, salir → saldr-. Then add the normal future (-é, -ás, -á, -emos, -án) or conditional (-ía, -ías, -ía, -íamos, -ían) endings.
teachings.rule: Most go verbs: infinitive vowel → d. Tener → tendr-. Venir → vendr-. Poner → pondr-. Salir → saldr-. Exception: traer is regular (traeré). Hacer → har-. Decir → dir-.

examples.spanish: Vendríamos más tarde, pero no tenemos tiempo
examples.english: We would come later, but we don't have time
Are you going out tonight? (present with future context)

teachings.notes: The -go in the present tense is an alarm bell that the verb will be irregular in the futures too. Traer is an exception — it stays regular.

teachings.summary: Hacer and decir are extra irregular in the future/conditional. Hacer → har- (haré, haría). Decir → dir- (diré, diría). Once you know one form, you can derive all the others.
teachings.rule: Hacer → har- (haré, harás, hará, haremos, harán / haría, harías, etc.). Decir → dir- (diré, dirás, etc. / diría, dirías, etc.).

examples.spanish: Lo haríamos, pero estamos ocupados ahora
examples.english: We would do it, but we're busy now
I tell you later (present with future context)

teachings.summary: Poder is not a go verb but also contracts in the future/conditional: the -e- falls out giving podr- (podré, podría). Similar contraction to haber → habr-.
teachings.rule: Poder → podr- (podré, podría). The /e/ falls out.

examples.english: I could / I would be able to
We could / we would be able to

teachings.notes: Subtle difference in sound: podría (I could) vs pondría (I would put).

teachings.summary: Review of future endings added to the infinitive: -é (I), -ás (you informal), -á (he/she/you formal), -emos (we), -án (they). These match the irregular present forms of haber.
Review of conditional endings added to the infinitive: -ía as the key sound, plus -mos, -n, -s for other persons.
teachings.notes: Remember 'me gustaría' (I would like) as the hook for the -ía sound.
